credit-card.notes: Ohio applies a 6-year limitation period to written contracts including credit card debt. Ohio's Consumer Sales Practices Act (CSPA) also provides additional protections against unfair debt collection.
credit-card.citation: Ohio Rev. Code § 2305.07; Ohio Rev. Code § 1345.01 et seq. (CSPA)

medical.notes: Medical debts in Ohio based on written contracts are subject to the 6-year limitation. Ohio enacted SB 225 (2022) providing medical debt relief programs.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances are subject to Ohio's 6-year written contract limitation.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Ohio are subject to the 6-year limitation period for written debt instruments.

student-loan.notes: Private student loans in Ohio are subject to the 6-year limitation. Federal loans are not subject to state SOL for government collection.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency judgments in Ohio are subject to the 6-year limitation from the date of the foreclosure sale.

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts in Ohio are subject to the standard 6-year limitation for written contracts.

regulatoryBody: Ohio Department of Commerce — Division of Financial Institutions & Ohio Attorney General
complaints: File a complaint with the Ohio AG at ohioattorneygeneral.gov or the Division of Financial Institutions at com.ohio.gov.
